extraction.one_line_summary: HINDLEY Jai won uphill sprint at Falls Creek after attacking 2.5km out, dropped everyone except HOWSON and BERRICK who led out finale.
tactical_patterns: Mitchelson-Scott waited until 13km to go before chasing down breakaway, allowing 24-year-old LYONS solo escape to persist dangerously.
MORTON's high-gear crushing attack style proved unsustainable on Falls Creek gradients despite ultra-endurance pedigree suggesting diesel power.
BERRICK as only non-WorldTour rider staying with favorites then leading out sprint reveals team tactical miscalculation losing stage.
Sunweb used STORER domestique work until 2.5km remaining before HINDLEY attacked, typical late-firing Australian team pattern.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: unexpectedly dropped when peloton still had numbers with 13km remaining, suggesting poor form or illness at supposed GC target race
rider_signals.BERRICK Sebastian: positive: only non-WorldTour rider staying with GC favorites and strong enough to lead sprint proves serious emerging talent
rider_signals.VINE Jay: neutral: bridged to MORTON attack but absorbed quickly, showing acceleration but not sustained climbing to threaten GC
rider_signals.LYONS Angus: positive: 24-year-old sustained solo attack from 55km out until final climb base, unusual diesel engine for young rider
rider_signals.MORTON Lachlan: negative: ultra-endurance background didn't translate to grinding climb power despite big gear mashing looking impressive visually
